jokes.text: Does Barry White want seconds?
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if Will can't wait for their parents to leave
jokes.punchline: Will uses Barry White reference to imply Carlton's obvious eagerness
jokes.explanation: Classic Will - uses pop culture reference with perfect timing and character voice. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Like Queen Elizabeth. Dan Quayle. Colonel Sanders.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains Hillary is a figurehead
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly absurd examples of figureheads including Colonel Sanders
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from legitimate to absurd. Perfect Carlton overthinking with pompous delivery.

jokes.text: Wonder you don't sneeze and pop your drawers.
jokes.setup: Will tells Carlton he's too tight/uptight
jokes.punchline: Vivid physical image of Carlton being literally too tight
jokes.explanation: Will's street smart way of describing Carlton's uptightness with colorful imagery.

jokes.text: I can't count the number of times I've gone swimming right after eating.
jokes.setup: Carlton claims he's not a stranger to life on the edge
jokes.punchline: His idea of dangerous is ignoring the swimming-after-eating rule
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - his sheltered idea of rebellion is hilariously mundane. Great ironic contrast.

jokes.text: Even Shelley Winters turned me down.
jokes.setup: Ashley complains about not selling cookies
jokes.punchline: References actress known for playing overweight characters who would presumably love cookies
jokes.explanation: Dark humor reference that's a bit mean-spirited but economical. Less character-specific than other jokes.

jokes.text: Could you please call my daddy? He's a lawyer. He'll know what to do.
jokes.setup: Will teaches Ashley a sales strategy involving fake injury
jokes.punchline: Reveals the scam involves threatening lawsuit through lawyer father
jokes.explanation: Classic Will street-smart scheming presented as helpful advice. Great misdirection structure.

jokes.text: I can't say it, because it would be a big fat lie.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil says he can't say he's looking forward to the legal convention
jokes.punchline: Admits he'd be lying if he said he was looking forward to it
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Phil's dry humor, but fairly standard structure.

jokes.text: Actually, we'll be reminiscing about Benson.
jokes.setup: Will asks what butlers do at conventions, mentioning Mr. French
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey corrects to Benson, the Black butler from TV
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-reference that acknowledges racial dynamics in TV butlers while staying character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Actually, we'll be reminiscing about Benson.
jokes.setup: Will asks about reminiscing about Mr. French
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey corrects - they reminisce about Benson, the Black butler
jokes.explanation: Smart correction that acknowledges race while maintaining humor. Geoffrey's dry delivery sells it perfectly.

jokes.text: Get It Your Own Damn Self.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentions this year's Black Butlers' Convention theme
jokes.punchline: Theme is rebellious anti-service message
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of butler stereotype with perfect economy. Highly quotable and character-driven.

jokes.text: This is a racial thing, right?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil gives stern warning about misbehavior
jokes.punchline: Will breaks fourth wall suggesting the lecture is racially motivated
jokes.explanation: Will's signature fourth-wall breaking with social commentary. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: I'm Bob Doomie.
jokes.setup: Hotel owner introduces himself at the Doomie Inn
jokes.punchline: His name matches the ominous hotel name
jokes.explanation: Simple name-based humor. Functional but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: And if you recall, the chariot bed didn't need horses to move it across the room.
jokes.setup: Phil mentions getting the same romantic suite from their anniversary
jokes.punchline: Implies vigorous sexual activity moved the bed
jokes.explanation: Mildly uncomfortable sexual innuendo delivered in Phil's pompous style. Solid character work.

jokes.text: Well, there's $50 says it isn't. / Thank you, sir, but his $200 says that it is.
jokes.setup: Phil offers $50 to get the Roman suite
jokes.punchline: Staff reveals the other guest offered $200
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation gag showing Phil being outbid. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: I'll do my Dance of the Seven Veils. And I didn't bring any.
jokes.setup: Vivian mentions doing the Dance of the Seven Veils
jokes.punchline: Reveals she didn't bring veils, implying nudity
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual innuendo from Aunt Viv. Slightly uncomfortable but fits romantic weekend setup.

jokes.text: Okay, kids, go to the movies.
jokes.setup: Parents getting increasingly sexual in their conversation
jokes.punchline: Phil abruptly sends kids away to continue privately
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat from responsible father suddenly realizing the conversation is inappropriate.

jokes.text: for a guy that's only been through the tenth grade once.
jokes.setup: Will compliments Tyrone on being smart
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment implying Tyrone is old for his grade
jokes.explanation: Classic Will backhanded compliment with perfect timing. Character-specific delivery style.

jokes.text: No, no, baby, these Canadian rules.
jokes.setup: Girl questions having to remove clothes in trivia game
jokes.punchline: Will lies about it being 'Canadian rules' to manipulate situation
jokes.explanation: Will's smooth manipulation with absurd excuse. Shows his street-smart scheming nature.

jokes.text: and don't come back through here until you're 17 or accompanied by an adult.
jokes.setup: Ashley interrupts Will's make-out session
jokes.punchline: Will sets ridiculous conditions for Ashley to return
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction to interruption with age-inappropriate humor. Shows Will's priorities.

jokes.text: All right, either of you ladies speak Latin?
jokes.setup: Will continues trying to impress the girls
jokes.punchline: Asks if they speak Latin in transparently fake intellectual move
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's transparent attempts to seem sophisticated. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Now get your tail over here, pronto. I want it fast and I want it hot. And if it has any pepperoni on it, I'm sending that puppy back!
jokes.setup: Carlton talks tough on phone about doing whatever he wants
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just ordering pizza with very specific demands
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - tries to sound tough but it's just pizza ordering. Great misdirection and character work.

jokes.text: I'm like a mother to these children
jokes.setup: Hillary explains why she should take the kids cookie selling
jokes.punchline: Claims maternal responsibility she clearly doesn't feel
jokes.explanation: Hillary's transparent attempt at responsibility. Character-appropriate irony but predictable.

jokes.text: Carlton, haul the brats. / I'm expecting company. / Yeah, I'll put it in the oven for you.
jokes.setup: Siblings argue about who should drive the kids
jokes.punchline: Will makes cooking innuendo about Carlton's 'company'
jokes.explanation: Rapid-fire sibling banter with mild sexual innuendo. Good energy but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Well, you're closest to the floor.
jokes.setup: Will says Carlton is closest to the door
jokes.punchline: Carlton retorts about Will's height
jokes.explanation: Standard height joke between the characters. Economical but predictable comeback.

jokes.text: I told you the 'R' didn't mean 'real fast.'
jokes.setup: Hillary and Ashley return disheveled from car crash
jokes.punchline: Will references Hillary's poor understanding of reverse gear
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Hillary's driving incompetence. Perfect character-based humor with good setup payoff.

jokes.text: I don't know, that's what Dad always says to me.
jokes.setup: Hillary asks what spending money has to do with the accident
jokes.punchline: She admits she just copied what her father usually says
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hillary moment showing her shallow understanding of parental authority. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: doesn't mean we should act like people without trust funds!
jokes.setup: Carlton gives speech about maintaining standards while parents are away
jokes.punchline: His standard is not acting like poor people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton privilege blindness delivered with complete sincerity. Excellent character-driven humor.

jokes.text: It's not so much my butt, man, it's Uncle Phil's foot coming towards it.
jokes.setup: Carlton says it's Will's butt if anything goes wrong
jokes.punchline: Will visualizes Uncle Phil's punishment method
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy reference establishing Will's fear of Uncle Phil's discipline. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: $5,000? Yo, man, I could purchase a new butt.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions they'll get $5,000 for the video shoot
jokes.punchline: Will jokes about buying a replacement butt for when Uncle Phil destroys his
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the previous butt joke with absurdist solution. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: And action!
jokes.setup: Director starts filming after Carlton's business explanation
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're filming a music video in the house
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment showing the scale of Carlton's mistake. Sets up the episode's main conflict.

jokes.text: Yeah, you got that shoe sale, right?
jokes.setup: Hillary mentions being busy and unable to do lunch
jokes.punchline: Performer assumes it's because of a shoe sale
jokes.explanation: Good character-based assumption about Hillary's priorities. Reinforces her shopping obsession.

jokes.text: Look, man, this is my house. You wanna be in a video, you go rob a liquor store.
jokes.setup: Tyrone keeps trying to get in the video
jokes.punchline: Will suggests criminal activity as alternative to music video fame
jokes.explanation: Classic Will street-smart humor with dark edge. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Did I say 'party machine'? I meant Banks residence. Master William speaking.
jokes.setup: Will answers phone as 'Party Machine' then realizes it might be his aunt
jokes.punchline: Quickly switches to overly formal butler impression
jokes.explanation: Good panic-driven misdirection showing Will's quick thinking under pressure. Nice character contrast.

jokes.text: Carlton's watching an educational documentary on loud music.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks about the loud music
jokes.punchline: Will claims it's an educational documentary about loud music
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist excuse with perfect delivery. Shows Will's quick-thinking lies under pressure.

jokes.text: That's the television. Your aunt's watching I Dream of Jeannie.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks about more music he hears
jokes.punchline: Will blames the sitcom theme song for the loud music
jokes.explanation: Follow-up lie that's increasingly absurd. Good escalation but less impactful than the first excuse.

jokes.text: Y'all ain't got to go home, but you gotta get the hell out of here.
jokes.setup: Will needs to clear everyone out before parents return
jokes.punchline: Uses classic bar closing line
jokes.explanation: Classic bar closing reference applied to house party situation. Recognizable and economical.

jokes.text: You ain't got to go home but you got to get the hell out of here.
jokes.setup: Carlton repeats Will's line when he realizes parents are coming home tonight
jokes.punchline: Carlton adopts Will's street language in panic
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Carlton adopting Will's language under stress. Character-driven repetition.

jokes.text: You ain't got to go home but you got to get the hell out of here.
jokes.setup: Carlton repeats Will's line
jokes.punchline: Carlton awkwardly adopts Will's street language
jokes.explanation: Funny hearing Carlton try to use Will's street expressions

jokes.text: The Titanic sank in one day! The Hindenburg blew up in one day! The entire city of Pompeii was covered in molten lava in one day!
jokes.setup: Will panics about Carlton's 'one day' reassurance
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly dramatic historical disasters that happened in one day
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating panic with perfect historical references. Excellent structure building to climax with Pompeii. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Well, I'm glad I don't live here. / Boy, I'm glad I don't live here.
jokes.setup: Friends see the destroyed house
jokes.punchline: Both express relief they don't live there
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat emphasizing the house's destruction. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Well, I'm glad I don't live here. / Boy, I'm glad I don't live here.
jokes.setup: Tyrone sees the disaster in the house
jokes.punchline: Repeats the same line for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Simple repetition for emphasis. Not particularly clever but serves as reaction to the chaos.

jokes.text: All you need is a little paint, a little elbow grease... everybody pulling together, I'm telling you the place is gonna look dope. / Oh, you know, I plan to, but not here. See, the fly honeys invited me to the wrap party.
jokes.setup: Tyrone gives encouraging speech about fixing the house together
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons them for a party with attractive women
jokes.explanation: Perfect fake-out showing Tyrone's true priorities. Great misdirection with character-consistent selfishness.

jokes.text: My God, Geoffrey needs to clean up around here.
jokes.setup: Hillary sees the completely destroyed house
jokes.punchline: Blames the mess on Geoffrey's housekeeping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hillary obliviousness to the obvious cause of destruction. Excellent character-driven irony.

jokes.text: I seem to recall a certain little sunshine trooper trying to get her car theft badge.
jokes.setup: Ashley refuses to help paint because she wasn't involved
jokes.punchline: Will reminds her she tried to get into the music video
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Girl Scout badge parody. Shows Will's ability to turn Ashley's argument against her.

jokes.text: I seem to recall a certain little sunshine trooper trying to get her car theft badge.
jokes.setup: Ashley refuses to help paint because she wasn't involved
jokes.punchline: Will reminds her she tried to get into the video earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ashley's earlier attempt to be in the video. 'Car theft badge' is a funny way to describe her participation.

jokes.text: Because I can't get it out.
jokes.setup: Philip says he's taking home the belly jewel
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's stuck to his body
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy showing Phil's romantic weekend gone wrong. Decent setup payoff but not memorable.

jokes.text: Caesar! It's time to invade Alexandria! / Coming, Alexandria!
jokes.setup: Parents maintain Roman roleplay despite hotel chaos
jokes.punchline: Continue elaborate historical romance fantasy
jokes.explanation: Awkward parental roleplay that sets up later callback. Mildly uncomfortable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: First, take a moment to familiarize yourself with your Buffomatic.
jokes.setup: Carlton insists on reading floor buffer instructions during crisis
jokes.punchline: Reads overly formal instruction manual language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton prioritizing rules over urgency. Good character consistency showing his methodical nature.

jokes.text: Make sure buffer is off before plugging in. / Do not use near water.
jokes.setup: Carlton reads safety instructions after Will already plugged in the buffer
jokes.punchline: Instructions warn against exactly what just happened
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with ironic timing. Carlton's rule-following backfires comedically.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's great. But when does the rest of it get here?
jokes.setup: Will comments on the replacement couch Hillary bought
jokes.punchline: Implies she only bought part of a couch
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag comment about Hillary's shopping incompetence. Economical and character-driven.

jokes.text: Don't let nobody tell you that big people can't wear plaid.
jokes.setup: Will returns from carpet-flying incident looking disheveled
jokes.punchline: Makes positive comment about his appearance despite obvious disaster
jokes.explanation: Classic Will maintaining confidence despite obvious failure. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: What'd you have, a Patti LaBelle nightmare?
jokes.setup: Carlton sees Will's disheveled appearance
jokes.punchline: References singer known for elaborate hair/styling
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits Carlton's knowledge base. Decent visual humor callback.

jokes.text: Relax, sweetheart. Burt Reynolds does it every morning on his way to work.
jokes.setup: Carlton complains about hanging onto rug at 90 mph
jokes.punchline: Will claims Burt Reynolds does this routinely
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist comfort attempt referencing Burt Reynolds' action movie persona. Character-appropriate encouragement.

jokes.text: I'm the idiot.
jokes.setup: Will mentions being stuck behind idiot with flying carpet
jokes.punchline: Carlton realizes and admits he was the idiot
jokes.explanation: Simple realization beat with good economy. Carlton's honest self-awareness in the moment.

jokes.text: We would have been here sooner, only we were stuck behind some idiot with a flying carpet. / I'm the idiot.
jokes.setup: Will complains about being stuck behind someone with a flying carpet
jokes.punchline: Carlton admits he was the idiot with the carpet on his car
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff. Will's complaint about traffic becomes personal when Carlton admits guilt. Great timing and economy.

jokes.text: Hey, very good, Carlton. That's the first step: admitting it.
jokes.setup: Carlton admits he's the idiot
jokes.punchline: Will treats it like addiction recovery program
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up joke treating Carlton's admission like therapy. Solid character interaction.

jokes.text: which means you each owe me $20.
jokes.setup: Carlton calculates they're $80 in debt after the disaster
jokes.punchline: Asks siblings to pay him for the privilege of destroying the house
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton business logic applied to disaster situation. Great irony showing his obliviousness to responsibility.

jokes.text: Well, y'all ready to read the Bible?
jokes.setup: Parents return home unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Will suggests immediate religious activity before punishment
jokes.explanation: Classic Will nervous deflection with religious humor. Shows his fear through character-appropriate joke attempt.

jokes.text: I'll get our bags packed. Ashley, you drive.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil seems pleased with how they maintained the house
jokes.punchline: Will prepares to flee with the youngest as getaway driver
jokes.explanation: Good panic reaction with absurd escape plan. Shows Will's street instincts kicking in.

jokes.text: Caesar! It's time to invade Alexandria! / Coming, Alexandria!
jokes.setup: Parents hear belly jewel story and continue roleplay
jokes.punchline: Resume Roman fantasy completely oblivious to house situation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that provides distraction from house disaster. Creates perfect ending confusion.

jokes.text: Caesar! It's time to invade Alexandria! / Coming, Alexandria!
jokes.setup: Phil and Vivian continue their Roman roleplay after discovering the house
jokes.punchline: They're so into their romantic weekend they ignore the disaster
jokes.explanation: Callback to hotel roleplay but doesn't add much new. The timing feels forced as episode ending.
